message:
test(e2e): add security headers integration tests

Create comprehensive Playwright tests for security headers:
- Test HSTS, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options headers
- Test Referrer-Policy and Permissions-Policy headers
- Validate Content-Security-Policy directives
- Test consistency across public pages and API routes
- Verify Stripe/Supabase/Plausible CSP allowances
- Ensure X-Powered-By header is absent

Closes: jeffreysprompts.com-5n0z

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Keep both repos in sync. ## Task Create integration tests that verify security headers and CSP behavior. ## Test Scenarios - HSTS, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options present - Referrer-Policy and Permissions-Policy set correctly - CSP header present and includes nonce (when enabled) - frame-ancestors enforced - No unsafe-eval unless explicitly required ## Endpoints to Test - / (public page) - /pricing (marketing) - /api/health (API route) ## Files to Create - tests/integration/security/headers.test.ts ## Success Criteria - Headers match configuration - CSP is valid and non-breaking - No regression on Stripe/Supabase domains ","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:18:55.526150585-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T01:18:55.526150585-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-5n0z","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-m61m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:25:31.55650821-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} +{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-5n0z","title":"Integration Tests: Security Headers & CSP","description":"## Origin Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-7ko9. Verified working with tests.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-5s2","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-esn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:37:42.840212232-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-5sh6","title":"Task: E2E test - Bundle discovery and exploration","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:46:47.300673254-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T19:55:59.311515968-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T19:55:59.311515968-05:00","close_reason":"Created comprehensive E2E tests for bundle discovery and exploration (22 tests covering page load, card interactions, detail page, navigation, responsive layouts, and 404 handling)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-5sh6","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-jxd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:56:49.641517998-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} @@ -326,7 +326,7 @@ {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-uskx","title":"Task: Markdown export tests (real file output)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:44:51.518919055-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T19:07:15.315777065-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T19:07:15.315777065-05:00","close_reason":"Core package tests already use real implementations (no mocks). 88 tests pass in packages/core.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-uskx","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-yhd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:55:11.408809788-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-uuy","title":"Build PromptGrid responsive component -- Details: CSS Grid layout, responsive columns (1→2→3→4 based on viewport). Responsive grid of PromptCard components. Supports category and tag filtering. Animates cards with staggered framer-motion. Empty state when no matches. 'Featured' badge on featured prompts. Mobile: 1 column. Tablet: 2 columns. Desktop: 3-4 columns. Lazy loads additional cards on scroll.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:46:57.221375236-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T02:02:51.98617945-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T02:02:51.98617945-05:00","close_reason":"PromptGrid component already exists with responsive grid (1-2-3 columns), skeleton loading state, empty state, and PromptCard rendering","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-uvi","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-oml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:49:38.25989114-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-uvi","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-vsk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:49:38.287569823-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} -{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-uw7z","title":"CLI: Add premium login command for authenticated access","description":"## Task Add a login command to the jfp CLI to authenticate with JeffreysPrompts Premium. ## Context The jfp CLI currently accesses only the static free-tier prompts. Premium users should be able to: - Authenticate their CLI to access their personal library - Sync saved prompts to local machine - Access premium-only content ## Implementation Use the device code flow (RFC 8628) that the premium project implements: 1. jfp login → opens browser to premium verification page 2. User confirms in browser 3. CLI receives token and stores securely 4. Subsequent commands include auth token ## Integration Points - Premium API: /api/cli/device-code (from premium project) - Premium API: /api/cli/token (from premium project) - Local storage: ~/.jeffreysprompts/credentials.json ## Commands - jfp login - Start device code flow - jfp logout - Clear stored credentials - jfp whoami - Show current authenticated user - jfp status - Show auth status and subscription tier ## Success Criteria - CLI can authenticate to premium API - Credentials stored securely - Token refresh handled automatically - Clear error messages for auth failures ## Cross-Repo Integration - Premium device code API endpoints: jeffreysprompts_premium-0o1 - Premium device code verification page: jeffreysprompts_premium-qdm - Premium CLI auth page (local browser flow): jeffreysprompts_premium-mn0 - Premium CLI JWT/token generation: jeffreysprompts_premium-qnl - Premium token refresh/revoke endpoints: jeffreysprompts_premium-0ve - Premium device_codes schema table: jeffreysprompts_premium-h8r - Premium CLI device code integration tests: jeffreysprompts_premium-wdao","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T00:30:26.577649305-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T02:41:55.988696608-05:00"} +{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-uw7z","title":"CLI: Add premium login command for authenticated access","description":"## Task Add a login command to the jfp CLI to authenticate with JeffreysPrompts Premium. ## Context The jfp CLI currently accesses only the static free-tier prompts. Premium users should be able to: - Authenticate their CLI to access their personal library - Sync saved prompts to local machine - Access premium-only content ## Implementation Use the device code flow (RFC 8628) that the premium project implements: 1. jfp login → opens browser to premium verification page 2. User confirms in browser 3. CLI receives token and stores securely 4. Subsequent commands include auth token ## Integration Points - Premium API: /api/cli/device-code (from premium project) - Premium API: /api/cli/token (from premium project) - Local storage: ~/.jeffreysprompts/credentials.json ## Commands - jfp login - Start device code flow - jfp logout - Clear stored credentials - jfp whoami - Show current authenticated user - jfp status - Show auth status and subscription tier ## Success Criteria - CLI can authenticate to premium API - Credentials stored securely - Token refresh handled automatically - Clear error messages for auth failures ## Cross-Repo Integration - Premium device code API endpoints: jeffreysprompts_premium-0o1 - Premium device code verification page: jeffreysprompts_premium-qdm - Premium CLI auth page (local browser flow): jeffreysprompts_premium-mn0 - Premium CLI JWT/token generation: jeffreysprompts_premium-qnl - Premium token refresh/revoke endpoints: jeffreysprompts_premium-0ve - Premium device_codes schema table: jeffreysprompts_premium-h8r - Premium CLI device code integration tests: jeffreysprompts_premium-wdao","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T00:30:26.577649305-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T03:20:49.142382389-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T03:20:49.142382389-05:00","close_reason":"Added auth status/tier info to jfp status output"} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-v2r0","title":"E2E Tests: Recently Viewed History","description":"## Origin Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-sco9. ## Task Create E2E tests for the recently viewed history feature. ## Test Scenarios 1. **History Recording** - Viewing prompt adds to history - Viewing collection adds to history - Viewing profile adds to history - Duplicate views update timestamp 2. **History Display** - Recent items show in sidebar/dropdown - Correct order (most recent first) - Item type indicators - Click navigates correctly 3. **History Management** - Clear all history - Remove single item - History respects max limit 4. **Privacy** - History is private - Not visible to others - Clear on logout option ## Logging Requirements - Log history additions - Log history access - Log clear actions - Screenshot history UI ## Files to Create - e2e/history/recently-viewed.spec.ts - e2e/lib/history-helpers.ts ## Success Criteria - History accurately recorded - UI displays correctly - Clear functions work - Privacy maintained ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:23:30.318372011-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T01:23:30.318372011-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-v2r0","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-d4t1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:25:35.545441905-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-v4p","title":"Add optional privacy analytics -- Details: Use Plausible or similar. Track: prompt_view, prompt_copy (no content), search (query length + result count, not actual query), export, skill_install. No PII. Optional - respect DNT.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:26:59.766502709-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T17:23:04.835363723-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T17:23:04.835363723-05:00","close_reason":"Added optional Plausible analytics + event tracking"} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-v6re","title":"Set up Sentry error tracking and monitoring","description":"## Origin Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-2rk. Keep both repos in sync. ## Task Set up Sentry for error tracking and performance monitoring. ## Why This Matters From AGENTS.md Deployment section: "Sentry for error reporting (optional)" - Catch errors in production before users report them - Track error frequency and impact - Monitor performance regressions - Debug issues with full context ## Implementation ### 1. Sentry Configuration ```typescript // sentry.client.config.ts import * as Sentry from "@sentry/nextjs"; Sentry.init({ dsn: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN, environment: process.env.NODE_ENV, tracesSampleRate: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production' ? 0.1 : 1.0, replaysSessionSampleRate: 0.1, replaysOnErrorSampleRate: 1.0, integrations: [ Sentry.replayIntegration(), ], beforeSend(event) { // Don't send events in development if (process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development') { return null; } return event; }, }); ``` ```typescript // sentry.server.config.ts import * as Sentry from "@sentry/nextjs"; Sentry.init({ dsn: process.env.SENTRY_DSN, environment: process.env.NODE_ENV, tracesSampleRate: 0.1, }); ``` ### 2. Error Boundary ```typescript // src/components/error-boundary.tsx "use client"; import * as Sentry from "@sentry/nextjs"; export function ErrorBoundary({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) { return ( (

Something went wrong

)} onError={(error) => { console.error('Caught error:', error); }} > {children} ); } ``` ### 3. Manual Error Capture ```typescript // In API routes or server actions try { await riskyOperation(); } catch (error) { Sentry.captureException(error, { tags: { component: 'billing' }, extra: { userId, action: 'checkout' }, }); throw error; } ``` ### 4. User Context ```typescript // After authentication Sentry.setUser({ id: user.id, email: user.email, subscription: user.tier, }); // On sign out Sentry.setUser(null); ``` ### 5. Performance Monitoring ```typescript // Custom spans for expensive operations const transaction = Sentry.startTransaction({ name: 'Search Query', op: 'search', }); const span = transaction.startChild({ op: 'db.query', description: 'Full-text search', }); // ... perform operation ... span.finish(); transaction.finish(); ``` ## Alert Configuration - Alert on new error types - Alert on error rate spike (>10/min) - Alert on performance regression (>2s p95) - Weekly digest of top errors ## Source Maps - Upload source maps during build - Associate with release version - Works with Vercel deployment ## Environment Variables ```env NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN=https://...@sentry.io/... SENTRY_DSN=https://...@sentry.io/... SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN=... (for source maps) SENTRY_ORG=jeffreysprompts SENTRY_PROJECT=premium ``` ## Files to Create - `sentry.client.config.ts` - `sentry.server.config.ts` - `sentry.edge.config.ts` - `src/components/error-boundary.tsx` - `next.config.ts` (add Sentry plugin) ## Dependencies ```json { "@sentry/nextjs": "^8.0.0" } ``` ## Success Criteria - Errors are captured in production - Source maps enable readable stack traces - User context is attached to errors - Performance is monitored - Alerts are configured ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:17:43.391275027-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T01:17:43.391275027-05:00"}
